arts.ttl: Arsenal full squad revealed for Monaco as Gabriel Magalhaes and Jurrien Timber decisions made
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta and Arsenal once more face a nervous wait over a host of key players with injuries continuing to cause major issues. Arteta must balance those ambitions with an increasingly difficult Premier League picture that has seen Chelsea move above his side and into second - after Fulham managed a draw with Arsenal on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Automatic Champions League progression will hand Arsenal huge bonus and a worst-case scenario
arts.sum: Arsenal are closing in on a Champions League knockout spot despite two full rounds of matches being left to play. The final group phase table will not be completed until the end of January, but with only one game to go before Christmas, things are looking up for Mikel Arteta.

arts.ttl: Every word Mikel Arteta said on Partey injury, Tierney return, Arsenal creativity and Jesus future
arts.sum: I’m very pleased with that, the way the team is adapting and the way players are making the effort to play in different positions. So when we face an incredibly good team like Monaco who is doing really well we are so used to it because we play opposition like that every week.

arts.ttl: Arsenal guaranteed to be without seven players vs Monaco amid Gabriel and Partey injury issues
arts.sum: "We need a cage there because he wants to go out, train and play," said Arteta. Giving a general update on his squad on Tuesday, Arteta said: "We have to manage a lot of the players."

arts.ttl: The three unorthodox Arsenal full-back picks Arteta can select for Monaco clash
arts.sum: With that in mind, football.london takes a look at the potential options Mikel Arteta has in the full-back roles that people are not expecting. "Yeah, he's ready and training really well and he's going to have an opportunity for sure the way the team is looking at the moment,” Arteta said about whether he is ready to play.

arts.ttl: Five of the best players to play for Arsenal and Monaco
arts.sum: Arsenal welcome AS Monaco to North London in the UEFA Champions League this evening. The Gunners have more in common with Monaco than just the red and white colours. Arsenal icon Arsene Wenger managed both clubs during an illustrious managerial career, while several stars have represented both teams. Ahead of their European encounter, we’ve remembered […] The post Five of the best players to play for Arsenal and Monaco first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Meet the player who just left Arsenal for £7m but is now breaking records at his new club this season
Bidding war? Chelsea ready to try again for €100m star who could also be subject of Arsenal bid
arts.sum: Chelsea are reportedly ready to try again for the potential transfer of in-form Barcelona winger Raphinha, though he could cost as much as €100million. The Brazil international took a bit of time to get going at Barca, but has been in electrifying form at the Nou Camp this season, reaching 16 goals and eight assists […] The post Bidding war? Chelsea ready to try again for €100m star who could also be subject of Arsenal bid appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: ‘My favourite player’: Thierry Henry says Arsenal have a player he prefers to Martin Odegaard
arts.sum: Arsenal have so many talented players on their books right now. Martin Odegaard is a world-class player, while William Saliba is arguably the world’s best...

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim 'believes Man United job is more complicated than he first thought' with honeymoon period well and truly over for new Red Devils boss following back-to-back defeats by Arsenal and Nottingham Forest
arts.sum: Any momentum in the sails of his arrival has been buffeted by back-to-back defeats by Arsenal and Nottingham Forest - only the second time this has happened in his career.
